title: How to 10x Your Claude Code Projects (Karpathy's Method)
summary: Austin Marchese presents three strategies attributed to Andrej Karpathy for improving developer output with Claude Code: LLM knowledge bases, auto-research, and context engineering. The video frames these as a replicable system for amplifying productivity with an AI code assistant.
writeup: A breakdown of Andrei Karpathy's viral post on LLM knowledge bases, distilling three compounding strategies for Claude Code: a structured wiki system (raw resources, an AI-maintained knowledge base, and a schema file), an auto-research loop where an agent proposes and evaluates changes autonomously, and context engineering via a well-scoped CLAUDE.md file. The through-line is removing the human as the bottleneck so each use of the tools accumulates value rather than starting from scratch. Karpathy's framing, 'the LLM is rediscovering knowledge from scratch on every question,' is the central diagnosis all three strategies answer.
key_points: Karpathy's LLM knowledge base is a three-layer structure: a read-only raw-resources folder (articles, transcripts, PDFs), an AI-maintained wiki that summarizes and cross-references them, and a schema file that instructs the model on conventions and health-check audits. Claude writes and updates the wiki; the human only adds raw sources.

The auto-research loop (from Karpathy's open-sourced project) cycles propose-test-evaluate-keep or discard without human prompting: running it on a small model training task yielded 20 stacked improvements and an 11% performance gain; the Shopify CEO ran 37 experiments overnight and achieved a 19% improvement.

Auto-research requires a measurable objective; for non-measurable tasks (reports, copy, app quality), the presenter adapts the loop by using chat-history back-and-forth as a proxy for output quality, feeding it into a knowledge-base-improvement skill rather than a numeric scorer.

Context engineering, Karpathy's term for filling the context window with precisely the right information, is the primary driver of output quality differences; the presenter's prescription is a CLAUDE.md under 50 lines stating the project structure, current build target, and common mistakes, combined with scoped rather than whole-codebase context.

Claude Code loops (recurring in-session commands) and schedules (cloud-run at a set time) are the built-in primitives for automating the improvement cycle; hooks are an alternative that trigger a command based on session events, such as reminding the user to run a knowledge-base improvement pass if it has not run recently.

chapters.title: Adapting Auto Research to Non-Measurable Work
